title: Denver 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: Denver 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Denver, United States: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Denver Art Museum and Denver Museum of Nature & Science. Prices from $2103 per person (estimate).
highlights: Hiking Red Rocks and nearby foothills trails
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Denver, United States — Semi-arid high-plains climate at 1,600m elevation with over 300 sunny days a year, cold snowy winters (highs 5-7°C) and warm dry summers (highs around 30°C) with big day-to-night temperature swings. June to September for hiking and mountain access with warm, dry days; December to March for skiing in nearby Rocky Mountain resorts. This itinerary blends Denver Art Museum, Denver Museum of Nature & Science, Red Rocks Park trails with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Denver Art Museum, Denver Museum of Nature & Science, Red Rocks Park trails
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Denver
itinerary.description: Land at Denver International Airport (DEN) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Denver Art Museum — Major fine art museum known for its Indigenous American collection. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Denver: Denver Museum of Nature & Science & Denver Zoo
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Denver Museum of Nature & Science — Natural history museum with an IMAX and planetarium. In the afternoon, continue to Denver Zoo — One of the country's older zoos with a strong conservation program. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Denver: Denver Botanic Gardens & Red Rocks Park & Amphitheatre visitor tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Denver Botanic Gardens — Extensive themed gardens in the heart of the city. In the afternoon, continue to Red Rocks Park & Amphitheatre visitor tour — Iconic red-sandstone concert venue and trails. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Red Rocks Park & Amphitheatre visitor tour

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Rocky Mountain National Park
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Rocky Mountain National Park, a popular side trip from Denver. Return to Denver in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Rocky Mountain National Park

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Meow Wolf Convergence Station — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Denver: Coors Field stadium tour & Molly Brown House Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Coors Field stadium tour — Behind-the-scenes tour of the Colorado Rockies' ballpark. In the afternoon, continue to Molly Brown House Museum — Home of Titanic survivor "Unsinkable" Molly Brown. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Red Rocks Park & Amphitheatre
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Red Rocks Park & Amphitheatre, a popular side trip from Denver. Return to Denver in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Red Rocks Park & Amphitheatre

itinerary.title: Denver: Downtown Aquarium Denver & Wings Over the Rockies Air & Space Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Downtown Aquarium Denver — Aquarium and dining complex with shark tanks. In the afternoon, continue to Wings Over the Rockies Air & Space Museum — Aviation and space history at a former Air Force base. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Wings Over the Rockies Air & Space Museum

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Cheesman Park skyline/mountain view — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Boulder
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Boulder, a popular side trip from Denver. Return to Denver in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Denver: Rockmount Ranch Wear/History of Denver walking tour & Meow Wolf Denver (Convergence Station)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Rockmount Ranch Wear/History of Denver walking tour — Guided historic downtown Denver walking tour. In the afternoon, continue to Meow Wolf Denver (Convergence Station) — Immersive surreal art experience across four fictional worlds. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Rockmount Ranch Wear/History of Denver walking tour

itinerary.title: Denver: Red Rocks Park trails & Larimer Square
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Red Rocks Park trails — Free hiking access to the dramatic sandstone formations outside shows. In the afternoon, continue to Larimer Square — Denver's oldest and most historic block, restored Victorian buildings. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Denver Firefighters Museum — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Civic Center Park or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to DEN for your departure flight.

travelTips: Give yourself a day to acclimatize to the mile-high elevation (1,609m) before strenuous hikes.
Book Red Rocks concert tickets or Rocky Mountain National Park timed-entry permits well in advance in summer.
The A Line train from DEN to Union Station is cheaper and often faster than a rideshare in traffic.
Afternoon thunderstorms are common June-August, especially in the mountains — plan hikes for early morning.
Alcohol hits harder at altitude — pace drinks the first day or two, especially if flying in from sea level.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
Sunscreen and sunglasses — high altitude UV is stronger even in cool weather
Layers for large day-to-night temperature swings
A refillable water bottle to counter dehydration at altitude
Warm winter gear if skiing or visiting December-March
thingsToDo: Red Rocks Park & Amphitheatre visitor tour
Wings Over the Rockies Air & Space Museum
Rockmount Ranch Wear/History of Denver walking tour
thingsToAvoid: Consuming cannabis edibles too quickly — effects take longer to kick in at altitude and are easy to overdo.
Public cannabis consumption — it's illegal outside private property despite legal retail sales.
Underestimating I-70 mountain traffic on winter ski weekends — delays of hours are common.
Hiking above treeline without checking afternoon thunderstorm timing — lightning risk is serious in summer.
Skipping hydration and sun protection, assuming cool temperatures mean low UV exposure.
topRestaurants: Snooze, an A.M. Eatery — American Brunch ($$)
Biker Jim's Gourmet Dogs — Gourmet Hot Dogs ($)
instagramSpots: Civic Center Park with mountain backdrop
adventureActivities: Hiking Red Rocks and nearby foothills trails
Day-trip skiing at Winter Park or Breckenridge
luxuryExperiences: Concert at Red Rocks with VIP seating
Fine dining tasting menu at Guard and Grace
Private ski helicopter transfer to a resort
Luxury stay at The Ritz-Carlton Denver
honeymoonExperiences: Romantic gondola or hot springs day trip to the mountains
Couples spa day with mountain views
budgetExperiences: Free hiking at Red Rocks Park
Free walk through Civic Center Park and the Capitol grounds

festivalCalendar.name: Denver Union Station Days / Underground Music Showcase

localEtiquette: Outdoor/athletic casual dress is the norm nearly everywhere, including many restaurants.
Tipping 18-20% at restaurants and bars is standard.
Cannabis is legal for adults 21+ but consumption is restricted to private property — public use is illegal and fined.
Locals are generally laid-back and outdoorsy; talking about hiking or ski conditions is easy small talk.
Altitude affects newcomers — locals won't be offended if you pace yourself on hikes or skip a second drink the first night.
localTransportGuide: The RTD A Line train connects Denver International Airport directly to Union Station downtown, and the free 16th Street Mall shuttle covers the core. Denver is otherwise fairly car-dependent for reaching the mountains or outlying neighborhoods, so renting a car is worthwhile if planning day trips to Red Rocks, Boulder or Rocky Mountain National Park.
bestTimeToVisit: June to September for hiking and mountain access with warm, dry days; December to March for skiing in nearby Rocky Mountain resorts.
weather: Semi-arid high-plains climate at 1,600m elevation with over 300 sunny days a year, cold snowy winters (highs 5-7°C) and warm dry summers (highs around 30°C) with big day-to-night temperature swings.
visaInformation: Visa-free ESTA entry for UK, EU, Australian and other Visa Waiver Program passport holders for stays up to 90 days. Indian nationals and most other nationalities require a US B1/B2 visitor visa arranged in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~14.7h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Denver?
faqs.answer: Visa-free ESTA entry for UK, EU, Australian and other Visa Waiver Program passport holders for stays up to 90 days. Indian nationals and most other nationalities require a US B1/B2 visitor visa arranged in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Denver?
faqs.answer: June to September for hiking and mountain access with warm, dry days; December to March for skiing in nearby Rocky Mountain resorts. Semi-arid high-plains climate at 1,600m elevation with over 300 sunny days a year, cold snowy winters (highs 5-7°C) and warm dry summers (highs around 30°C) with big day-to-night temperature swings.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Denver safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Denver is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Denver?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Sarah Andersen
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Denver is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Union Station. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Priya Khan
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Cherry Creek Trail in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Arjun Rossi
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Denver Zoo in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Fatima Khan
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Meow Wolf Denver (Convergence Station) alone was worth the trip, but combined with Rockmount Ranch Wear/History of Denver walking tour this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Marco Ivanov
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Denver Zoo and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Marco Williams
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
